description_long: This object is a large, robust mortar and pestle, traditionally used for grinding and mixing medicinal substances. The mortar is deep and bowl-shaped with a wide, flared rim and sits on a thick, stable base. The pestle is long and cylindrical, featuring decorative bands and a bulbous midsection for a comfortable grip. The entire set appears to be made from a single material, likely a heavy metal such as brass or bronze, giving it a solid, weighty appearance. The surface is matte with a slightly rough texture, showing subtle wear and patina typical of frequent use. There are no visible labels, logos, or text on the object, and it is devoid of any modern packaging features.
description: A large, heavy brass or bronze mortar and pestle with a deep bowl and a long, decorated pestle. The surface is matte and slightly rough, showing signs of traditional use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full circular opening of the mortar and the pestle standing upright in the center.
description_view_1: Side view with the pestle leaning at an angle, revealing the depth and thickness of the mortar's walls.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the pestle upright and the mortar's base and rim clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the pestle angled, highlighting the interior and the flared rim of the mortar.
